description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a pale, off-white coloration and a somewhat bulbous, organic form. Its surface is matte and slightly rough, with subtle undulations and rounded protrusions that suggest natural weathering. The stone lacks any sharp edges, instead featuring smooth transitions between its various lobes and depressions. There are no visible carvings, inscriptions, or artificial modifications, giving it a raw, natural appearance. The coloration is mostly uniform, with faint hints of gray and beige, and the texture appears consistent across all sides. This stone would be substantial in weight and size, making it a prominent obstacle or feature in an urban landscape.
description: A large, pale, irregularly rounded stone with a matte, rough surface and subtle natural undulations. Its organic shape and uniform color make it a natural-looking urban barrier.
description_view_0: From this angle, the stone appears to have two prominent rounded lobes in the foreground and a third, slightly smaller lobe at the back.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated, undulating profile of the stone, with two main bulges and a gentle dip between them.
description_view_2: Here, the stone looks more compact and blocky, with a single broad surface facing forward and less pronounced lobes.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the stone's asymmetry, showing a deep groove and a pronounced ridge along one side.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on pavement or grass
attribute_pattern: No distinct pattern; surface is mostly uniform with subtle mottling and natural undulations.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and slightly worn, indicating moderate exposure to weather but no heavy erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts gently with man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements nearby.
